Ramashish Koeri gang.description: A caste-based dacoit band active in the Kaimur plateau region of Bihar, India, during the 1980s. Los Zetas.description: Los Zetas were a Mexican drug cartel founded in 1997 by Osiel Cárdenas Guillén, originally as an armed wing of the Gulf Cartel. The cartel was known for extreme violence and was considered a major rival to the Sinaloa Cartel. Birmingham Boys.description: The Birmingham Boys were a street gang active in England from the 1910s to the 1930s, founded by Billy Kimber. They controlled racecourses in the Midlands and North, and later expanded to London, engaging in bookmaking, assault, extortion, and other crimes. They lost control of South East racecourses to the Sabini gang after a series of violent conflicts.

Savage Skulls.description: The Savage Skulls are a mostly Puerto Rican and African American street gang founded in the Hunts Point area of the Bronx in the late 1960s. They gained popularity in the 1970s and declared war on drug dealers in their territory. The gang was involved in conflicts with rival gangs and was documented in a 1979 film. They are known for appropriating Nazi symbolism.

Bailey Boys.description: A street gang based in Buffalo, New York, founded around 2011 and named after Bailey Avenue. The gang is involved in murder, robbery, drug trafficking, and racketeering. It gained notoriety after a 2012 shooting in Martin Luther King Jr. Park. In 2018, leader Kenneth Pettway Jr. was arrested on federal drug and RICO charges, with 14 members indicted.

Blekingegade Gang.description: Danish far-left criminal group active from 1972 to 1989, which committed robberies to finance the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine (PFLP). The group was known for its extreme secrecy, meticulous planning, and use of disguises. One police officer was killed during their final robbery in 1988. The gang was disbanded after the discovery of a weapons cache on Blekingegade in 1989.

Black Spades.description: The Black Spades is a mostly African-American street gang that originated in the Bronx, New York, in the late 1960s. It gained popularity in the 1970s and later spread to other parts of New York City and states like Massachusetts and Connecticut. In 2019, the gang rebranded as TBS New Direction, a community service group focusing on anti-violence and food distribution. The gang was influenced by the Five-Percent Nation, Malcolm X, and the Black Panthers.
